issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly by dividing the failed_count by total_count for each row, instead of calculating it once per transaction_date.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: WITH failed_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date), total_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS total_count FROM fact_transactions GROUP BY transaction_date) SELECT t.transaction_date, ft.failed_count, tt.total_count, (ft.failed_count::DECIMAL / tt.total_count) * 100 AS failure_rate FROM total_transactions tt LEFT JOIN failed_transactions ft ON tt.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had correctness issues with the failure rate calculation, performance issues with the correlated subquery, and readability issues due to lack of comments.
